definitions.definition: The ability to perform daily tasks and sports activities efficiently and effectively. It involves physical, mental, and emotional well-being.
A combination of cardiorespiratory endurance, muscular strength, muscular endurance, flexibility, and body composition.
Training that focuses on developing skills and abilities required for a specific sport.
A training approach that involves dividing the training year into specific periods or phases, each with a specific goal or focus.
A condition that occurs when an athlete trains too frequently or intensely, leading to physical and mental exhaustion.
A condition that occurs when the body is unable to withstand the demands of training or competition, resulting in damage to tissues or organs.
A process that helps an athlete recover from an injury or illness, and return to training or competition.
The study of how athletes can use food and nutrients to achieve optimal performance and recovery.
The process of returning the body to its normal state after exercise or competition.
A series of measures that can be taken to reduce the risk of injury during training or competition.
The amount of physical and mental stress imposed on the body during training.
The process of improving physical fitness through exercise and training.
The process of preparing an athlete mentally for competition, including techniques such as visualization and positive self-talk.
The internal drive or desire to participate in physical activity and achieve success in sport.
The ability to work effectively with others towards a common goal.
The process of preparing an athlete strategically for competition, including analysis of opponents and development of game plans.
The proper form and execution of a specific movement or skill.
